rationale: This page contains significant factual information regarding the Key Provisions of the CEC and Other Election Commissioners Act, 2023, including qualifications, appointment committees, and removal procedures. It also details the ECINET digital platform and postal ballot counting rules, which are highly relevant for polity and governance exams.
cleanedText:
• Motion of removal then must be passed by a majority of two-thirds members present and voting in both Houses of Parliament.
• After that, President of India passes an order removing the CEC.
Key Provisions of CEC and Other Election Commissioners Act, 2023
Qualifications
• For CEC and EC, the person:
- Must hold/have held post equivalent to Secretary to Government of India.
- Must possess expertise in managing and conducting elections.
Search Committee
• Leadership: Headed by Minister of Law and Justice.
• Composition: Two members (Secretary rank or higher).
• Function: Prepares panel of 5 candidates.
Select Committee
• Members:
- Prime Minister (Chairperson).
- Leader of Opposition (Member).
- Union Cabinet Minister nominated by PM (Member).
• Function: Recommends candidates to President.
Resignation and Removal
• Resignation Process: CEC or EC can resign by writing to President.
• Removal Conditions:
- CEC: Like Supreme Court Judge.
- ECs: Requires CEC recommendation.
Term of Office
• Duration: 6 years or until 65 years of age, whichever earlier.
• Terms and Conditions:
- If an EC becomes the CEC, combined tenure cannot exceed 6 years.
- No reappointment allowed.
5.4. KEY TERMS/INITIATIVES IN NEWS
5.4.1. ECINET DIGITAL PLATFORM
ECI used the new ECINET digital platform during recent bye-elections in five Assembly constituencies.
About ECINET app
• One-stop digital platform for timely voter turnout reporting.
- Under Conduct of Elections Rules, 1961 (framed under RPA, 1951), Presiding Officers must provide Form 17C to booth-level agents after polls.
- Form 17C records polling station wise voter turnout details (electors, votes cast/denied/not recorded) and candidate-wise total votes polled.
- Its Rule 49B revised to improve EVM ballot paper readability, including colour photographs of candidates.
• Key Features
- Presiding Officers update voter turnout data every two hours on polling day.
- Only designated EC officials can enter data to ensure accuracy.
- Faster Index Card Publication in 72 hours of results.
- Index Cards are non-statutory post-election reports providing constituency-level data on candidates, voters, votes, party shares, gender voting patterns, and regional variations.
5.4.2. POSTAL BALLOT
According to ECI’s notification, penultimate round of EVM counting shall be taken up only after complete counting of Postal Ballots.
